title: teucrium disjunctum
intro: Teucrium disjunctum, also known as the alpine germander, is a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It is native to the mountainous regions of the Mediterranean and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and fever. The plant's essential oil, which contains compounds like borneol and borneol acetate, has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, offering relief from various health issues such as nausea, headaches, and skin problems. Herbal preparations of Teucrium disjunctum include tea, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits and medicinal properties.
